name: cand-gate-growth-on-food-engine-0
notes: Replaces the unconditional "grow family as early as housing allows" with growth gated on a renewable food engine that scales to mouths; adds the +2-food-per-mouth-forever framing and the no-cooker-no-animal-food rule. Only the strategy block changes.

blocks.strategy: Strategy basics: feed the family first — begging is -3 each and decides games. Every family member you have permanently raises your food bill by 2 food at EVERY remaining harvest (a newborn costs 1 the harvest it is born, 2 after); 5 harvests remain after round 4, so an extra mouth in stage 2 can cost ~10 food across the game. Never add a mouth (family growth) or commit actions to extra rooms until you already have a RENEWABLE food engine that covers the bigger bill: either (a) a sown field you re-sow each cycle (sowing fills a field with 3 grain or 2 veg; re-sowing needs no re-plow), or (b) a cooking improvement (Fireplace 2-3 clay, then Cooking Hearth 4-5 clay) PLUS a breeding pair (2+ of an animal type) housed in a pasture or stables so they breed every harvest. One-shot food actions (Day Laborer, Fishing, Traveling Players, Resource Market) only patch the current harvest — they do not scale with mouths, so do not let them justify growing. Build the engine first: aim for a cooking improvement and your first field by the end of stage 1-2, then grow. Never hold animals without a cooking improvement to convert them — raw animals are 0 food (grain and vegetables each convert to 1 food raw anytime, so crops are a safer food buffer than uncooked animals). At each feeding, before accepting any begging, convert every spare grain, vegetable, and (if you have a cooker) every spare animal beyond your breeding pairs. Don't leave whole scoring categories at -1, and convert spare resources into points and filled spaces late.
